subject: Cloud Computing
title: Service Accounts and Machine Identity
description: A quick win: understand Service Accounts and Machine Identity.

hook.text: Humans aren't the only ones with cloud access. Applications, scripts, and services need identities too.

buildup.text: Service accounts are non-human identities that let applications authenticate to cloud resources.

discovery.text: Access keys and tokens grant service accounts permission. They're like passwords, but for machines.

twist.text: Leaked service account keys are the number one cause of cloud breaches. They end up in code repos constantly.

climax.text: Best practice: use temporary credentials that expire automatically. Never hardcode secrets in source code.

punchline.text: Machine identities need security too. They're often the weakest link.

quiz.question: What is the top cause of cloud security breaches related to service accounts?
options: Access keys accidentally leaked in code repositories
Service accounts that expire too quickly
Cloud providers sharing service account keys publicly
